pr.title: Mass rename scene callback functions from scene tables
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Mass semantic rename of scene callback functions and related scene-table data symbols. The PR changed 40 files with balanced additions/deletions, primarily updating config/GALE01/symbols.txt plus matching C stub markers and header prototypes so address-based names now carry callback-role suffixes such as _OnEnter, _OnFrame, _OnLeave, _OnLoad, _OnUnload, and _OnInit. Evidence indicates this was a naming-only refactor: the decomp.dev GALE01 report said "No changes".
what_changed: Renamed many global scene callback symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt while preserving the same .text addresses and sizes, e.g. gm_8016D32C to gm_8016D32C_OnFrame, gm_8016E934 to gm_8016E934_OnEnter, gm_80177704 to gm_80177704_OnLeave, and gm_801737E8 to gm_801737E8_OnLoad.
Updated source-side nonmatching function markers to the new names, for example /// #gm_801A0A10 became /// #gm_801A0A10_OnEnter and /// #gm_801A0B18 became /// #gm_801A0B18_OnLeave.

Updated header prototypes to keep declarations aligned with renamed symbols, for example gm_801A0A10_OnEnter, gm_801A0B18_OnLeave, gm_801A0C6C_OnEnter, and gm_801A0E0C_OnLeave in src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.h.
Renamed at least one implemented empty function, changing void gm_801A5614(void) {} to void gm_801A5614_OnUnload(void) {}.

Applied the callback-role convention across multiple subsystems, including gm, mn, ty, un, and vi files: examples include mnStageSel_8025A998_OnEnter, mnStageSel_8025B850_OnFrame, mnStageSel_8025BB5C_OnLeave, mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter, mnCharSel_802669F4_OnFrame, and mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave.

Also renamed scene-table data objects from bare address symbols to names ending in _MinorScenes, such as gm_803DD6A0_MinorScenes, gm_803DD6D0_MinorScenes, gm_803DD888_MinorScenes, and gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes.
smart_moves: Used the scene tables as semantic evidence for callback roles instead of leaving opaque address-only names.
Kept all addresses, sizes, and scopes intact in symbols.txt, making the rename audit-friendly and low-risk.
Performed the rename consistently across symbols.txt, C files, and headers in the same PR, reducing stale prototype or nonmatching-marker drift.
Chose compact suffixes that encode lifecycle/callback role while preserving the existing address-based prefix, which keeps symbols searchable by address and by role.
Validated as a no-binary-change cleanup: the automated GALE01 report for the PR stated "No changes".
decomp_lessons: When a function is known only by address but appears in a scene table callback slot, prefer appending the slot role to the existing address symbol rather than inventing behavior-specific names too early.
For mass renames, update config symbols, header declarations, source definitions, and /// # nonmatching labels together so decomp tooling and references remain synchronized.
Lifecycle suffixes are useful breadcrumbs for future matching because they document call context even before function bodies are decompiled.
No-op or tiny callback functions should still receive role names when their table slot is known, as seen with the OnUnload-style rename.
Scene-table data arrays themselves benefit from semantic suffixes such as _MinorScenes; this helps distinguish data tables from generic local objects or byte blobs.
naming_conventions: Address-preserving function names use the existing module/address prefix plus PascalCase callback suffix: _OnEnter, _OnFrame, _OnLeave, _OnLoad, _OnUnload, _OnInit.

Examples of retained prefix plus role suffix include gm_8016D32C_OnFrame, gm_8016E934_OnEnter, gmCamera_801A34FC_OnFrame, gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter, mnStageSel_8025BB5C_OnLeave, and vi0102_8031D000_OnFrame.
Scene/minor-scene table data symbols use an address-based prefix plus _MinorScenes, e.g. gm_803DD6D0_MinorScenes.
The PR preserved existing module prefixes such as gm, gmTitle, gmCamera, mn, mnStageSel, mnCharSel, un, vi, toy, and tyFigupon rather than normalizing them into one namespace.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a symbol/prototype rename pass rather than an assembly matching change; no evidence of codegen tactics, asm edits, or function-body matching changes appears in the provided diff excerpt.
The matching-relevant tactic was keeping renamed symbols address-identical in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and receiving a decomp.dev GALE01 "No changes" report.
Address-based names were retained inside renamed identifiers, preserving grepability for future matching work while adding semantic callback-role information.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was from decomp-dev[bot]: "Report for GALE01 (3b00a3f - 0edaebe)\n\nNo changes", supporting that the PR was binary-neutral.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile functions renamed with _OnEnter/_OnFrame/_OnLeave in PR 1602.
Search for remaining scene callback functions in scene tables that still lack lifecycle suffixes.
Search for _MinorScenes data symbols to identify scene table structure work after this rename.
Check whether future naming conventions keep PascalCase callback suffixes or migrate to another style.
